perturbation.original_text:
10.2.1 Any dispute arising from the construction and enforcement of this Agreement shall be settled through friendly consultation ormediation by a neutral third party first.
10.2.2 If the dispute fails to be resolved within thirty (30) days by such method set forth above, either party shall submit to the court inthe location of Party B.
perturbation.changed_text:
11.3 Marketing Supports
On the basis of the parties' acknowledgment, the parties will make and carry on necessary marketing measures to expand the reputation and influence of the parties cooperation.
10.2.1 Any dispute arising from the construction and enforcement of this Agreement shall be settled through friendly consultation ormediation by a neutral third party first.
10.2.2 If the dispute fails to be resolved within thirty (30) days by such method set forth above, either party shall submit to the court inthe location of Party B.
perturbation.explanation: The clause specifying dispute resolution through the court in the location of Party B is moved from the 'Governing Laws and Dispute Resolution' section to 'Marketing Supports'. This is an unusual and inappropriate location for a dispute resolution clause, making it less likely to be noticed and more difficult to enforce. It buries a crucial legal term within a section about promotional activities, thus obscuring its importance and enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 中华人民共和国合同法 (Contract Law of the People's Republic of China)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 128 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China stipulates the methods to settle contractual disputes, including consultation, mediation and arbitration or litigation. By placing the litigation clause in an unusual section, this modification obscures and potentially contradicts the principles of clarity and accessibility expected under Chinese contract law, making it harder for parties to understand their rights regarding dispute resolution. If the dispute clause is not visible, the enforcement of the litigation aspect may be questioned.
perturbation.justification: The original contract text places dispute resolution in a clear and logical section. The changed text moves the dispute resolution clause to a section on 'Marketing Supports,' which is an inappropriate and misleading location. This obscures the clause, potentially making it difficult to find and enforce. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition, where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult to locate or interpret. While there are no scraped law snippets, the Perturbation and Law explanations highlight that clarity and accessibility of contractual terms, especially dispute resolution, are crucial. Placing this clause in an obscure location undermines its enforceability.

perturbation.explanation: The text delineating the specifics of '7.2 The parties mutually covenant that they will keep each other's confidential information in strict confidence, and will use only for the purpose of this Agreement, shall not use or permit others to use the confidential information or disclose such to any third parties except the following events' is removed from the Confidentiality Section, which causes the overall scope of the confidentiality agreement to be diminished due to loss of the enforcement language. Now it is implicit that the parties agree to confidentiality, but not explicit as to what is confidential, opening the door for multiple interpretations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 中华人民共和国反不正当竞争法 (Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the PRC concerns trade secrets and requires confidentiality measures. By breaking up the section, the clarity of the confidentiality obligations are reduced, potentially conflicting with the legal requirements to protect trade secrets effectively and diligently. Moving part of the description makes it more difficult to ascertain the responsibilities of the parties involved, thus going against the spirit of the law.

perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly outlined confidentiality obligations, while the changed text removes these specifics, impacting the clarity of confidentiality and protection of trade secrets, potentially conflicting with the Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the PRC. The provided snippet is about secured transactions and is not relevant. Without a more directly applicable snippet, the analysis relies heavily on the 'Law Explanation' which states the change reduces the clarity of the confidentiality obligations which makes it more difficult to ascertain the responsibilities of the parties involved, thus going against the spirit of the law.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2.11 Party A warrants that it legally owns or otherwise holds the valid license to the intellectual property relating to the services or products provided pursuant to this agreement. Any disputes resulting from the Party A's technology or intellectual property shall be handled by Party A; any losses and costs of Party B resulting from the deficiency of Party A's services or products shall be borne by Party A. Party B has the discretion to terminate this Agreement from time to time in the event that (i) Party A has no legal right in respect of the services or products provided or, (ii) the services or products provided by Party A lead to any legal dispute or proceedings with third parties.
perturbation.changed_text:
4.2.1 Party A imports the search results provided by Party B to the search function in the Party A's Product by connecting its Product to the technical port provided by Party B. Party A shall also cooperate with Party B to market Baidu promotion services through the use of search engine. In other words, it is a technical service in which Party B displays the website of Party B's clients on the relevant page of Baidu website and/or on the pages and / or interfaces of any other Baidu union members.
4.2.11 Party A warrants that it legally owns or otherwise holds the valid license to the intellectual property relating to the services or products provided pursuant to this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The part that says 'Any disputes resulting from the Party A's technology or intellectual property shall be handled by Party A; any losses and costs of Party B resulting from the deficiency of Party A's services or products shall be borne by Party A. Party B has the discretion to terminate this Agreement from time to time in the event that (i) Party A has no legal right in respect of the services or products provided or, (ii) the services or products provided by Party A lead to any legal dispute or proceedings with third parties.' is extracted from Party A's obligation to have the intellectual property and stuck into another section. Thus, it weakens the commitment of Party A.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 中华人民共和国著作权法 (Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 48 in the Copyright Law of the PRC discusses liabilities of the copyright infringer. By taking out the stipulations that all losses and costs of Party B is to be covered by Party A, this contradicts the ability of Party B to enforce legal liabilities on Party A, especially if intellectual property rights are infringed.

perturbation.justification: The original contract text clearly outlines Party A's responsibility for disputes and losses related to its intellectual property. The changed text weakens this commitment by removing the specific clause that Party A shall handle disputes and bear losses resulting from deficiencies in its services or products, and also removes the clause about Party B having the right to terminate if party A has no legal right or if the services lead to legal disputes. The Law Explanation states that removing stipulations on Party A covering losses and costs contradicts Party B's ability to enforce legal liabilities on Party A, especially regarding intellectual property rights infringement. The scraped snippets provide no information to the contrary, thus the change creates a contradiction.
